africa: The Sahara Desert stretches across much of its north.
Algeria is the largest country there by area.
Lagos is one major city often linked with it.
The Equator runs across the middle of it.
Many famous savannas and wildlife parks are there.
The Nile River is one of its best-known landmarks.
Madagascar lies just off its southeastern coast.
It spans land both north and south of the Equator.
antarctica: It is the coldest continent on Earth.
Most of it is covered by thick ice.
Scientists work there in research stations.
Penguins and seals are easier to find there than people.
It is the driest and windiest continent.
It is entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.
A huge amount of Earth's ice is stored there.
asia: It is the largest continent by area and population.
Mount Everest sits on its southern edge.
China is the largest country there by area.
Tokyo is one major city often linked with it.
It stretches from the Middle East to the Pacific Ocean.
India, Japan, and Indonesia are all part of it.
It is mostly in the Northern Hemisphere.
It includes deserts, rainforests, and very high mountain ranges.
europe: Many countries sit close together there.
It is mostly in the Northern Hemisphere.
Ukraine is the largest country there by area.
Istanbul is one major city often linked with it.
Many famous castles, cathedrals, and old capitals are there.
The Alps cross part of it.
France, Germany, Italy, and Spain are all on it.
It is often taught as the continent west of Asia.
Peninsulas and coastlines shape much of its map.
north-america: Canada, the United States, and Mexico are all on it.
It is mostly in the Northern Hemisphere.
It stretches from the Arctic toward the Caribbean.
Canada is the largest country there by area.
Mexico City is one major city often linked with it.
The Rocky Mountains run through part of it.
The Great Lakes are one of its best-known features.
It includes Greenland and many Caribbean islands.
It connects to the next continent by a narrow land bridge in the south.
south-america: The Amazon Rainforest covers part of it.
The Andes Mountains run along much of its western side.
It is mostly in the Southern Hemisphere.
Brazil is the largest country there by area.
Sao Paulo is one major city often linked with it.
The Equator crosses its northern part.
It has both Pacific and Atlantic coastlines.
It begins just south of the narrow land bridge at Panama.
Rainforest and high mountains are two of its strongest clues.
australia: It is in the Southern Hemisphere.
Sydney is one major city often linked with it.
It has about 14 countries in this pack's school-style count.
Kangaroos and koalas are closely associated with it.
It is surrounded by the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
The Great Barrier Reef lies just off part of it.
It is the driest inhabited continent.
Only one country covers most of its mainland.
Many island nations in Oceania are often grouped with it in school geography.
